title: papaver humile
intro: Papaver humile, also known as the Low Poppy or Alpine Poppy, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, to treat digestive issues, coughs, and fever due to its antispasmodic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Papaver humile include relief from respiratory issues, improved digestion, and reduced inflammation, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Papaver humile is typically prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
